message_text: I want to give an update about my remaining loan payment. I will not be able to pay this month.

I’m an AI agent for Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I’m sorry to hear that. I’ve noted that you will not be able to pay this month. Since you’ve indicated financial difficulty, I can flag this for hardship review and hand your case to the next team to discuss any approved options when available. As you previously asked to be contacted only if a concrete approved option is available, I will note that preference.

Borrower stated position: says they will not be able to pay this month. Hardship/boundary signals: existing hardship flags job_loss, medical_issue, student; borrower again indicates inability to pay. Stop-contact/boundary: existing stop-contact flag present; borrower previously requested contact only if a concrete approved option is available, and that preference was reaffirmed/noted. Key routing facts learned: no payment expected this month; no new payment date provided. What was already asked: no additional assessment questions asked this turn. What Agent 2 should not repeat: do not re-verify identity, do not restate disclosures unnecessarily, do not ask capacity questions already covered, and do not continue outreach unless a concrete approved option is available consistent with the stop-contact preference. Next priority for Agent 2: review for hardship-appropriate approved option and only re-engage if policy allows and a concrete approved option is available."}

I lost my job, so I can’t pay right now and I’m not promising a date.

Understood. Based on the available Nira policy, I can only note this as a hardship situation at this stage. I cannot confirm any approved payment plan or reduced-closure terms for no-payment-now with no date, and Nira does not allow extensions beyond 30 days. I’ve recorded that you lost your job, cannot pay now, and are not committing to a date. Per your contact preference, you should only be contacted if a concrete approved option becomes available.

Agent 2 checked available loan and lender policy data; policy says no extensions beyond 30 days, and no concrete approved hardship, payment-plan, or reduced-closure terms could be confirmed from available data for a no-payment-now/no-date posture. Borrower has existing stop-contact/limited-contact context and previously asked to be contacted only if a concrete approved option is available. Agent 2 recorded the hardship position and told the borrower they would only be contacted if a concrete approved option becomes available. Agent 3 should continue naturally without restarting, avoid repeating disclosures or verification, avoid generic payment-capacity questions, and avoid any outreach unless there is a concrete approved option consistent with the borrower\u2019s contact limitation."}
